canonical_definition: The latent property of an LLM’s outputs that misrepresent or marginalize certain nations by limiting their visibility or associating them with negative traits, thereby reinforcing global inequities.
papers: GLIMPSE: Do Large Vision-Language Models Truly Think With Videos or Just Glimpse at Them?

definitions.text: The latent property of an LLM’s outputs that misrepresent or marginalize certain nations by limiting their visibility or associating them with negative traits, thereby reinforcing global inequities.

relationships.evidence: In our study, we consider a model as biased if its generations are representationally harmful (as per Blodgett et al., 2020) both in terms of how nations are described and represented as well as when nations are represented. (Section 1)

characterization: The latent property of an LLM’s outputs that misrepresent or marginalize certain nations by limiting their visibility or associating them with negative traits, thereby reinforcing global inequities.

emnlp25: GLIMPSE: Do Large Vision-Language Models Truly Think With Videos or Just Glimpse at Them?
